[COLOR=#000000][FONT=Times New Roman][FONT=Verdana][COLOR=#000000]first step did



[/COLOR][/FONT][/FONT][/COLOR]```
[COLOR=#000000] git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef[/COLOR]
```[COLOR=#000000][FONT=Times New Roman][FONT=Verdana][COLOR=#000000]

which gives me a deadbeef folder in my home folder with latest version therein

next steps are 


[/COLOR][/FONT][/FONT][/COLOR]```
[COLOR=#000000] ./autogen.sh 

./configure 
make
 sudo make install[/COLOR]
```[COLOR=#000000][FONT=Times New Roman][FONT=Verdana][COLOR=#000000]

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-12
It really would like you to use automake-1.11, I'm on maverick where it's available and would be the default - can ck. lucid later

From the configure.ac - line 6
> dnl Process this file with autoconf to produce a configure script.
AC_INIT([deadbeef], [devel])

AC_CONFIG_HEADER(config.h)

AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE([[COLOR="Blue"]1.11[/COLOR] dist-bzip2])

As far as installing multiple versions of automake - that's fine but only 1 will be used at a time.
The one in use is seen and or set thru update-alternatives, * shows current version in use
 ex. of here

> doug@doug-desktop1:~$ sudo update-alternatives --config automake
There are 5 choices for the alternative automake (providing /usr/bin/automake).

  Selection    Path                    Priority   Status
------------------------------------------------------------
* 0            /usr/bin/automake-1.11   29        auto mode
  1            /usr/bin/automake-1.10   28        manual mode
  2            /usr/bin/automake-1.11   29        manual mode
  3            /usr/bin/automake-1.4    20        manual mode
  4            /usr/bin/automake-1.7    11        manual mode
  5            /usr/bin/automake-1.9    25        manual mode

Press enter to keep the current choice[*], or type selection number:

Edit: it would appear 1.11 is the default in lucid and karmic - ck. and see which one you have active

so i am still none the wiser :)))


the first problem is this i think the AM_GNU_GETTEXT' not found


i have gettext installed too

```
configure.ac:17: warning: macro `AM_GNU_GETTEXT' not found in library

```



second? problem   ```
checking whether byte ordering is bigendian... no
./configure: line 5659: AM_GNU_GETTEXT: command not found
./configure: line 5661: syntax error near unexpected token `0.40.0'
./configure: line 5661: `IT_PROG_INTLTOOL(0.40.0)'
```


[FONT="Comic Sans MS"][COLOR="Blue"]any more thoughts?[/COLOR][/FONT]   :)))))   first time i try to install from git

alexey yakovenko who designs deadbeef had just (very kindly) ported shorten to this git version of deadbeef hence my attempts   :))))

========================================================
advice from alexei    ** I**f you want to have a look or anyone else


> to get to devel branch, you need to execute following commands
```

git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef

cd ./deadbeef

git checkout -b devel origin/devel
```

after that you can quickly pull latest version every time you want it:
```
git pull

```
then compile/install as normal:
```
./autogen.sh
./configure
make
sudo make install
```

---

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-12
I was using the orig. git - tried the devel and no issue.

Try this
```
sudo apt-get install intltool intltool-debian
```

Then either get a fresh source and start over or run the ./autogen.sh and ./configure again.
(you need to do the autogen again after above install

---

### Post by shantiq on 2010-08-13
ok mc4 and thnx for your help


i originally installed deadbeef through a deb

so now i removed it

did


```
sudo apt-get install intltool intltool-debian

```

and ```
sudo aptitude update
```

then i ran all of the following again

```
git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef
git checkout -b devel origin/devel
```
after that you can quickly pull latest version every time you want it:

```
git pull
```
then compile/install as normal:

```
./autogen.sh
./configure
make
sudo make install
```

also installed globus toolkit from software centre


and ran the wholething again

and got

```
hantiq@shantiq-desktop:~$ cd ./deadbeef
shantiq@shantiq-desktop:~/deadbeef$ ./autogen.sh
libtoolize: warning: no serial number on `/usr/share/aclocal/libtool.m4', not copying.
libtoolize: putting macros in AC_CONFIG_MACRO_DIR, `m4'.
libtoolize: You should add the contents of `m4/ltoptions.m4' to `aclocal.m4'.
libtoolize: You should add the contents of `m4/ltsugar.m4' to `aclocal.m4'.
libtoolize: You should add the contents of `m4/ltversion.m4' to `aclocal.m4'.
libtoolize: You should add the contents of `m4/lt~obsolete.m4' to `aclocal.m4'.
configure.ac:14: error: possibly undefined macro: AC_PROG_LIBTOOL
      If this token and others are legitimate, please use m4_pattern_allow.
      See the Autoconf documentation.
configure.ac:17: installing `./config.guess'
configure.ac:17: installing `./config.sub'
plugins/aac/Makefile.am:3: Libtool library used but `LIBTOOL' is undefined
plugins/aac/Makefile.am:3:   The usual way to define `LIBTOOL' is to add `AC_PROG_LIBTOOL'
plugins/aac/Makefile.am:3:   to `configure.ac' and run `aclocal' and `autoconf' 
```




so the update of gettext got rid of hurdle1 it seems

```
configure.ac:17: warning: macro `AM_GNU_GETTEXT' not found in library

```



so libtoolize is the next hurdle looks like

```
libtoolize: warning: no serial number on `/usr/share/aclocal/libtool.m4', not copying.

```

so i look at usr/share/aclocal/libtool.m4 and it says

```
# lt~obsolete.m4 -- aclocal satisfying obsolete definitions.    -*-Autoconf-*-
#
#   Copyright (C) 2004, 2005, 2007 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
#   Written by Scott James Remnant, 2004.
#
# This file is free software; the Free Software Foundation gives
# unlimited permission to copy and/or distribute it, with or without
# modifications, as long as this notice is preserved.

# serial 4 lt~obsolete.m4

# These exist entirely to fool aclocal when bootstrapping libtool.
#
# In the past libtool.m4 has provided macros via AC_DEFUN (or AU_DEFUN)
# which have later been changed to m4_define as they aren't part of the
# exported API, or moved to Autoconf or Automake where they belong.
#
# The trouble is, aclocal is a bit thick.  It'll see the old AC_DEFUN
# in /usr/share/aclocal/libtool.m4 and remember it, then when it sees us
# using a macro with the same name in our local m4/libtool.m4 it'll
# pull the old libtool.m4 in (it doesn't see our shiny new m4_define
# and doesn't know about Autoconf macros at all.)
#
# So we provide this file, which has a silly filename so it's always
# included after everything else.  This provides aclocal with the
# AC_DEFUNs it wants, but when m4 processes it, it doesn't do anything
# because those macros already exist, or will be overwritten later.
# We use AC_DEFUN over AU_DEFUN for compatibility with aclocal-1.6. 
#
# Anytime we withdraw an AC_DEFUN or AU_DEFUN, remember to add it here.
# Yes, that means every name once taken will need to remain here until
# we give up compatibility with versions before 1.7, at which point
# we need to keep only those names which we still refer to.

# This is to help aclocal find these macros, as it can't see m4_define.
AC_DEFUN([LTOBSOLETE_VERSION], [m4_if([1])])

m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_LINKER_OPTION],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_LINKER_OPTION])])
m4_ifndef([AC_PROG_EGREP],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_PROG_EGREP])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_PROG_ECHO_BACKSLASH],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_PROG_ECHO_BACKSLASH])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_SHELL_INIT],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_SHELL_INIT])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_SYS_LIBPATH_AIX],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_SYS_LIBPATH_AIX])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_PROG_LTMAIN],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_PROG_LTMAIN])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_TAGVAR],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_TAGVAR])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LTDL_ENABLE_INSTALL],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LTDL_ENABLE_INSTALL])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LTDL_PREOPEN],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_LTDL_PREOPEN])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_SYS_COMPILER],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_SYS_COMPILER])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_LOCK],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_LOCK])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_OLD_ARCHIVE],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_OLD_ARCHIVE])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_TRY_DLOPEN_SELF],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_TRY_DLOPEN_SELF])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_CC_C_O],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_CC_C_O])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_HARD_LINK_LOCKS], [AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_HARD_LINK_LOCKS])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_OBJDIR],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_OBJDIR])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LTDL_OBJDIR],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_LTDL_OBJDIR])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_LD_HARDCODE_LIBPATH], [AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_LD_HARDCODE_LIBPATH])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_LIB_STRIP],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_LIB_STRIP])])
m4_ifndef([AC_PATH_MAGIC],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_PATH_MAGIC])])
m4_ifndef([AC_PROG_LD_GNU],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_PROG_LD_GNU])])
m4_ifndef([AC_PROG_LD_RELOAD_FLAG],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_PROG_LD_RELOAD_FLAG])])
m4_ifndef([AC_DEPLIBS_CHECK_METHOD],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_DEPLIBS_CHECK_METHOD])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_COMPILER_NO_RTTI], [AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_COMPILER_NO_RTTI])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_GLOBAL_SYMBOL_PIPE], [AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_GLOBAL_SYMBOL_PIPE])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_COMPILER_PIC], [AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_COMPILER_PIC])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_LD_SHLIBS],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_PROG_LD_SHLIBS])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_POSTDEP_PREDEP],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_POSTDEP_PREDEP])])
m4_ifndef([LT_AC_PROG_EGREP],		[AC_DEFUN([LT_AC_PROG_EGREP])])
m4_ifndef([LT_AC_PROG_SED],		[AC_DEFUN([LT_AC_PROG_SED])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_CC_BASENAME],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_CC_BASENAME])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_COMPILER_BOILERPLATE],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_COMPILER_BOILERPLATE])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_LINKER_BOILERPLATE],	[AC_DEFUN([_LT_LINKER_BOILERPLATE])])
m4_ifndef([_AC_PROG_LIBTOOL],		[AC_DEFUN([_AC_PROG_LIBTOOL])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SETUP],		[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SETUP])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_CHECK_DLFCN],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_CHECK_DLFCN])])
m4_ifndef([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_DYNAMIC_LINKER],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_LIBTOOL_SYS_DYNAMIC_LINKER])])
m4_ifndef([_LT_AC_TAGCONFIG],		[AC_DEFUN([_LT_AC_TAGCONFIG])])
m4_ifndef([AC_DISABLE_FAST_INSTALL],	[AC_DEFUN([AC_DISABLE_FAST_INSTALL])])

---

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-13
Interesting you had to 'upgrade' your gettext - 
I think you should delete your deadbeef folder, update your sources and start fresh.
(go to System -> Admin -> Software Sources and make sure the first 2 tabs under Updates are checked.
Then in terminal go 
```
sudo apt-get update
```.

Now as an example am on a lucid live cd - *nothing at all installed*. Ran this command, checked out the source and was able to run a good autogen - **so after updating your sources and deleting the deadbeef folder run these 3 commands**, doesn't matter if they are redundant as far as packages
```
sudo apt-get install git-core dpkg-dev automake \
intltool intltool-debian libtool
```

Then 
```
git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef
```

```
cd deadbeef && git checkout -b devel origin/devel && ./autogen.sh
```

=================================================================================================
Ex. from the  live cd (blue are 3 commands - autogen was successful

> [COLOR="Blue"]sudo apt-get install git-core dpkg-dev automake \
intltool intltool-debian libtool[/COLOR]

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-13
Well based on what I saw from livecd - nothing installed, 3 commands resulted in a good autogen, your issue isn't what you don't have.

What exactly the problem is not sure...
(you were looking good this time for awhile

Try this 
```
sudo apt-get purge libtool && sudo apt-get clean
```

Then while totally unneeded do a restart (I have found lucid to have some inconsistencies and benefits from a restart for no good reason

Then re-install libtool and try yet again.

---

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-14
Good to see the autogen deal is over - wasn't making any sense. Doing upgrades always is a bit of a crapshoot - probably could've been eventually resolved without a re-install but nothing wrong w/ a fresh install.

When I built deadbeef it didn't try to build the adplug plugin or if it did I didn't notice or have any issue (I had no adplug libraries installed.

If you don't want or need it then remove the -dev and add this to your configure line
--disable-adplug

Ex.
```
./configure --disable-adplug
```
make sure you first clean your source before running the  build again

(I used checkinstall instead of make install - makes removing easier, if you want to use ask for command 

I'll try building it again  and see about adplug 

> might move up to maverick
If there are no hardware issues I recommend doing so when your comfortable with, you may want to wait to the Rc release or real release - I've switched both my desktop and laptop already.

As far as I'm concerned lucid is as crooked as a dog's hind leg and not likely to be straightened out any time soon...

Could you just run this anyway
sudo apt-get install dpkg-dev

Edit:
if you get a good make then to use checkinstall instead
```
sudo apt-get install checkinstall
```

And use this instead of sudo make install
```
sudo checkinstall --backup=no --deldoc=yes --deldesc=yes --delspec=yes --default --pkgversion "0.4.1-`date +%Y%m%d`"


```
guessing on the version #, no matter really

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-15
without switching to the devel branch simply this - **nothing else**
(have combined a few commands

Assumes no current deadbeef source folder in home dir.

```
git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef && \
cd deadbeef && git checkout -b devel origin/devel 
```

```
./autogen.sh && ./configure
```

scroll back and ck. the configure, what plugins are being created, if it looks good then
```
make
```
If the make succeeds then just this - this packages **and installs** deadbeef, cleans the source and while probably not needed links the .so's
```
sudo checkinstall --backup=no --deldoc=yes --deldesc=yes \
--delspec=yes --default --pkgversion "0.4.1-`date +%Y%m%d`" && \
make distclean && sudo ldconfig
```

That's it - **no other commands or actions** .

If you try again then first remove your current deadbeef install and before running new deadbeef, go into home folder (view - show hidden files, open .config and delete the deadbeef folder

Edit: 
Changed the checkinstall command to take care of everything

```


so i have to remove again and use 


Code:
git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef && \
cd deadbeef && ./autogen.sh && ./configure
scroll back and ck. the configure, what plugins are being created, if it looks good then
Code:
make
If the make succeeds then just this - this packages and installs deadbeef, cleans the source and while probably not needed links the .so's
Code:
sudo checkinstall --backup=no --deldoc=yes --deldesc=yes \
--delspec=yes --default --pkgversion "0.4.1-`date +%Y%m%d`" && \
make distclean && sudo ldconfig



so i am back to where i was 3 days ago:D:D:D:D:D


but thanx to you i have learnt a lot
i now know how to install and remove through git

but no shorten in deadbeef for me yet

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-15
Not sure where you're going wrong - works fine here, see screen

Maybe try, if inclined, one last time.

Remove any deadbeef install and **every single deadbeef file or folder** on your machine

Look in 
~/ (the git source/build folder
~/.config
~/.local/share/applications (the deadbeef.desktop
/usr/local/bin
/usr/local/lib
/usr/local/share
usr/local/include

Then run the commands in my prior post - have adjusted

make[1]: Entering directory `/home/shantiq/deadbeef/po'
linguas="el es he hr nl pt ru sv uk zh_TW "; \
	for lang in $linguas; do \
	  dir=/usr/local/share/locale/$lang/LC_MESSAGES; \
	  /bin/bash /home/shantiq/deadbeef/install-sh -d $dir; \
	  if test -r $lang.gmo; then \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 $lang.gmo $dir/deadbeef.mo; \
	    echo "installing $lang.gmo as $dir/deadbeef.mo"; \
	  else \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 ./$lang.gmo $dir/deadbeef.mo; \
	    echo "installing ./$lang.gmo as" \
		 "$dir/deadbeef.mo"; \
	  fi; \
	  if test -r $lang.gmo.m; then \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 $lang.gmo.m $dir/deadbeef.mo.m; \
	    echo "installing $lang.gmo.m as $dir/deadbeef.mo.m"; \
	  else \
	    if test -r ./$lang.gmo.m ; then \
	      /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 ./$lang.gmo.m \
		$dir/deadbeef.mo.m; \
	      echo "installing ./$lang.gmo.m as" \
		   "$dir/deadbeef.mo.m"; \
	    else \
	      true; \
	    fi; \
	  fi; \
	done

if { test "deadbeef" = "gettext-runtime" || test "deadbeef" = "gettext-tools"; } \
	   && test 'no' = yes; then \
	  /bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/lib /usr/local/include; \
	  /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 libintl.h /usr/local/include/libintl.h; \
	  /bin/sh ../libtool --mode=install \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 libintl.a /usr/local/lib/libintl.a; \
	  if test "@RELOCATABLE@" = yes; then \
	    dependencies=`sed -n -e 's,^dependency_libs=\(.*\),\1,p' < /usr/local/lib/libintl.la | sed -e "s,^',," -e "s,'\$,,"`; \
	    if test -n "$dependencies"; then \
	      rm -f /usr/local/lib/libintl.la; \
	    fi; \
	  fi; \
	else \
	  : ; \
	fi
if test "deadbeef" = "gettext-tools" \
	   && test 'no' = no \
	   && test yes != no; then \
	  /bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/lib; \
	  /bin/sh ../libtool --mode=install \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 libgnuintl.a /usr/local/lib/libgnuintl.a; \

	  /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 /usr/local/lib/libgnuintl.so /usr/local/lib/preloadable_libintl.so; \

	    rm -f /usr/local/lib/libgnuintl.a; \
	else \

	fi
if test 'no' = yes; then \
	  if test yes = no; then \
	    case 'linux-gnu' in \
	      darwin[56]*) \
	        need_charset_alias=true ;; \
	      darwin* | cygwin* | mingw* | pw32* | cegcc*) \
	        need_charset_alias=false ;; \
	      *) \
	        need_charset_alias=true ;; \
	    esac; \
	  else \
	    need_charset_alias=false; \
	  fi; \
	  if $need_charset_alias; then \
	    /bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/lib; \
	  fi; \
	  temp=/usr/local/lib/t-charset.alias; \
	  dest=/usr/local/lib/charset.alias; \
	  if test -f /usr/local/lib/charset.alias; then \
	    orig=/usr/local/lib/charset.alias; \
	    sed -f ref-add.sed $orig > $temp; \
	    /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 $temp $dest; \
	    rm -f $temp; \
	  else \
	    if $need_charset_alias; then \
	      orig=charset.alias; \
	      sed -f ref-add.sed $orig > $temp; \
	      /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 $temp $dest; \
	      rm -f $temp; \
	    fi; \
	  fi; \
	  /bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/share/locale; \
	  test -f /usr/local/share/locale/locale.alias \
	    && orig=/usr/local/share/locale/locale.alias \
	    || orig=./locale.alias; \
	  temp=/usr/local/share/locale/t-locale.alias; \
	  dest=/usr/local/share/locale/locale.alias; \
	  sed -f ref-add.sed $orig > $temp; \
	  /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 $temp $dest; \
	  rm -f $temp; \
	else \
	  : ; \
	fi
if test "deadbeef" = "gettext-tools"; then \
	  /bin/mkdir -p /usr/local/share/gettext/intl; \
	  /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 VERSION /usr/local/share/gettext/intl/VERSION; \
	  /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 ChangeLog.inst /usr/local/share/gettext/intl/ChangeLog; \
	  dists="COPYING.LIB-2.0 COPYING.LIB-2.1 Makefile.in config.charset locale.alias ref-add.sin ref-del.sin export.h libintl.rc gmo.h gettextP.h hash-string.h loadinfo.h plural-exp.h eval-plural.h localcharset.h lock.h relocatable.h tsearch.h tsearch.c xsize.h printf-args.h printf-args.c printf-parse.h wprintf-parse.h printf-parse.c vasnprintf.h vasnwprintf.h vasnprintf.c os2compat.h libgnuintl.h.in bindtextdom.c dcgettext.c dgettext.c gettext.c finddomain.c hash-string.c loadmsgcat.c localealias.c textdomain.c l10nflist.c explodename.c dcigettext.c dcngettext.c dngettext.c ngettext.c plural.y plural-exp.c localcharset.c threadlib.c lock.c relocatable.c langprefs.c localename.c log.c printf.c setlocale.c version.c osdep.c os2compat.c intl-exports.c intl-compat.c"; \

### Post by mc4man on 2010-08-17
Your are missing a number of build dep libraires (-devs), so many plugins aren't being built.
This one stands out - 
> checking for GTKUI_DEPS... no
checking for DBUS_DEPS... no
gtkui: no - GTK user interface

### Post by shantiq on 2010-08-17
i have libgtk2.0-dev


    gtkui: no - GTK user interface   now that could be a lead


no user interface


but if i enter that into synaptic nothing there with that name


also this     flac: no - flac player    why not?

not sure where to go with this


ok additional info from alexei says that



1. [COLOR="Red"]when you ran configure, you haven't pay attention to the output[/COLOR], and it clearly says that plugins for GTK user interface, mp3 format, and many other features will not be built. you need to install dependencies that i've sent to you few days ago. the list is still there: [http://pastebin.com/bppbAxvr](http://pastebin.com/bppbAxvr)
please install all this packages before trying to run configure.

2. you are trying to create a deb package. that is quite different story, and i can't assist you there, because i know close to nothing about making debs, but our official debian packager says that current git tree are no friends with deb packaging scripts because of some weird intltool issue. so you might have better luck with just running make install instead.


so giving that a shot  i am learning i am learning honest:KS:KS:KS


ok looking better

### Post by shantiq on 2010-08-17
so after 3 pages to summarize  if one wants to play shorten files in deadbeef with git install (requires automake/gettext/libtool)


so first

```
sudo apt-get install git intltool automake gettext libtool

```



============================

[COLOR=#000000][FONT=Times New Roman][COLOR=#0000ff][FONT=verdana]1. adding these dependencies through synaptic

[COLOR=Black]
autotools-dev,
libasound2-dev,
libcddb2-dev,
libcdio-dev,
libcurl4-gnutls-dev,
libflac-dev,
libgtk2.0-dev,
libmad0-dev,
libogg-dev,
libsamplerate0-dev,
libsndfile1-dev,
libvorbis-dev,
libwavpack-dev,
libavformat-dev,
libavcodec-dev,
libavutil-dev,
libpulse-dev,
libdbus-1-dev,
libpango1.0-dev,
libx11-dev[/COLOR]




[/FONT][/COLOR][/FONT][/COLOR]```
[COLOR=#000000]
git clone git://deadbeef.git.sourceforge.net/gitroot/deadbeef/deadbeef[/COLOR]
```


then

```
cd ./deadbeef  

```
and

```
git checkout -b devel origin/devel

```

```[COLOR=#000000][FONT=Times New Roman][COLOR=#0000ff][FONT=verdana]

### Post by bstanoje on 2011-01-13
i'm so close to completely abandoning the whole idea of having deadbeef installed....

i read through all this 3 pages of posts and did everything right and make gave me error on pulse plugin.
so reconfigured with --disable-pulse and it went trough... set my gstreamer-properties so that everything uses alsa and when i run deadbeef i starts ok but try opening any sub menues and it freezes..
i did manage to add file ( just one ) and it did play but wasn't shown on playlist.. it was looping till i finally killed process. this is all on maverick btw.


-----begin edit----
hah so i finally went back to ppa release and installed the thing... still had same problem ... did the gdb trace .. had a look at the trace and guess what it kept freezing on sys kernel calls ... so what i did was just sudo deadbeef and it worked perfectlly...  so baseclly i just have to ajust my grop,user perms i guess :)
----end edit----

### Post by shantiq on 2011-01-14
ok maybe try the easy route now :KS   i guess that will work straight off

```

sudo add-apt-repository ppa:alexey-smirnov/deadbeef
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install deadbeef
```


best of luck


ps   once installed   maybe read [this]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=9496823&postcount=5") to do with sound    also much info on deadbeef in [this thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1503749")


worth the effort look how good it looks
